root_text.an9.92:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāmesu avītarāgo hoti …pe…
root_text.an9.92:2.1: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya ime cattāro iddhipādā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.an9.92:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu chandasamādhipadhānasaṅkhārasamannāgataṁ iddhipādaṁ bhāveti,
root_text.an9.92:2.7: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ cetasovinibandhānaṁ pahānāya ime cattāro iddhipādā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.an9.92:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.92:1.3: Firstly, a mendicant isn’t free of greed for sensual pleasures. …
translation_text.an9.92:1.4: These are the five shackles of the heart.
translation_text.an9.92:2.1: To give up these five shackles of the heart you should develop the four bases of psychic power.
translation_text.an9.92:2.3: It’s when a mendicant develops the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to enthusiasm, and active effort.
translation_text.an9.92:2.4: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to energy, and active effort.
translation_text.an9.92:2.5: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to mental development, and active effort.
translation_text.an9.92:2.6: They develop the basis of psychic power that has immersion due to inquiry, and active effort.
translation_text.an9.92:2.7: To give up these five shackles of the heart you should develop these four bases of psychic power.”
